meta.title: GDP shares (US$) and the price of gold — MeasuringWorth
meta.method: Nominal GDP per country converted to US$ at contemporaneous market FX, then each as a share of the four-economy total. UK via Dollar-Pound (US$/£); Spain euro-series via 166.386 ptas/€ and the peseta/US$ rate; Australia nominal = real x deflator, then A$/US$ (pre-1966 £A x2).
meta.share_window_note: Each year's denominator is the sum of whatever economies are US$-convertible that year, so the stack composition changes: US from 1790, UK from 1791, Spain from 1900, Australia from 1928 (entry set by FX availability). Pre-1791 UK GDP exists (1700+) but has no US$ FX.

sources.us_gdp: Williamson, 'What Was the U.S. GDP Then?', measuringworth.com/usgdp
sources.uk_gdp: Thomas & Williamson, 'What Was the U.K. GDP Then?' (Consistent Series), measuringworth.com/ukgdp
sources.spain_gdp: Prados-de-la-Escosura, 'What Was Spain's GDP Then?', measuringworth.com/datasets/spaingdp
sources.australia_gdp: Hutchinson & Ploeckl, 'What Was the Australian GDP or CPI Then?', measuringworth.com/datasets/australiadata
sources.gold: Officer & Williamson, 'The Price of Gold, 1257-Present' (NY market US$/oz), measuringworth.com/gold
sources.fx_global: Officer, 'Exchange Rates Between the USD and Forty-one Currencies', measuringworth.com/datasets/exchangeglobal
